item.title: This SCP is brought to you by the number +100
item.content:encoded: Well that was quick. I think this is the fastest any SCP of mine, outside of x000 entries, has gotten to +100. Thanks to all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one. When it dawned on me that this was basically the Challenger in another universe where they managed to fit Bit Bird on, it made this all the much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how this gives just enough clues for the reader to start piecing together what may have happened, while still leaving us with no definitive answers. The impression I got was that the Muppets from this other universe were real, living organisms, not just puppets. When they were transferred to our world they weren't compatible with the local reality, and so they were made to fit. In most cases, this resulted in an inanimate puppet containing that soup of human tissue; but since Big Bird in our world is a costume instead of a puppet, he was unfortunate enough to survive the transformation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Look at how they massacred my boy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it the interdimensional shift that caused the fusion with the suit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4481-A was the sole surviving crew member aboard SCP-4481. SCP-4481-A is a costume resembling the character of "Big Bird" from Sesame Street, albeit with several discrepancies.

Why did nobody tell me about this!!??? I'm such a sucker for puppet/costume related SCPs.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved this from the jump thanks to knowing that bit about Bird almost getting sent into space. This is such a great cross of series 1 + 2 vibes. Thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so confused and uncomfortable.

+1

EDIT: Read the spoilers in your comment. Less confused, more uncomfortable. Stronger +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt uncomfortable and unsettled reading this.

Thanks, I hate it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was merely enjoying this, then when I got near the end, it all just… clicked in my head… and then, I REALLY enjoyed it. Wow!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both are made by the Jim Henson company, and the puppets on Sesame Street are generally referred to as Muppets. Plus, Kermit the Frog debuted on Sesame Street.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice. My only comment on this is, aren't Sesame Street and The Muppets two different and separate things?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is only in the Special Edition which includes deleted scenes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t remember this part of Muppets from Space.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is what I get for doing Find+Replace while dyslexic.

Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, but the number throughout is SCP-4881 instead of SCP-4481.

item.title: Can you tell me how to get, how to get to Author Commentary?
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers!
Wowee go spoiler ursefl

So, yeah. I banged this out in around half an hour last night after watching a video by Sam O'Nella Academy] dealing with the history of animals in space. As an aside, he mentions the fact that Big Bird was almost going to be sent into space, suit and all, as a publicity stunt. NASA found out that they couldn't fit him into the shuttle, so they went with a teacher instead.

And then the Challenger exploded.

The title of this comes from a quote from that video— "There is a timeline not too far from this one where Big Bird is a casualty in the single worst astronautical disaster in history".

The SCP number is, as of writing, the number of episodes of Sesame Street that have aired.

Thanks to everyone who looked this over on chat, including ARD, AKFrost, LordOfLaugh, Fitnessgram, Crashington, and Grimmcreeper, for making sure this wasn't a complete coldpost.

Can't believe this is the first thing that I've posted in months, though.
